evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java is 988 lines and combines: HTTP server lifecycle, route registration for 10+ endpoints, per-request parameter parsing/validation, business-logic dispatch, and JSON serialization — at least 4 distinct responsibilities in one class.

JSON serialization is copy-pasted as Map<String,Object> building blocks in every handler method (handleFullSketch, handleFullDependencySketch, handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticField, handleConcordanceExamples) — a repeated structural pattern that should be data-driven or use typed response DTOs.

handleFullSketch and handleFullDependencySketch are near-identical in structure (iterate relations, executeSurfacePattern, build collocations list, put into response map) but diverge only in the response key names ('patterns' vs 'relations') — the structural duplication was not extracted.

handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti both contain a 15-line block of parameter parsing (relationId resolution, top/min_shared/min_logdice parsing with NumberFormatException guard) that is copy-pasted verbatim across both handlers.

handleVisualRadial and handleBcqlQueryPost duplicate OPTIONS/method-check logic that registerGetHandler already encapsulates for GET endpoints — because POST handlers bypass registerGetHandler, the CORS guard is re-implemented inline.

start() both registers all routes AND starts the server in the same method — a TODO comment (595bb84c) acknowledges this should be split; this creates a method with two responsibilities that cannot be called independently.

Result DTOs (ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge) are inner classes of SemanticFieldExplorer, creating an awkward import chain in WordSketchApiServer that is acknowledged in a TODO (28bc8918/a430f585) but unresolved.

BlackLabQueryExecutor (537 lines) mixes: BlackLab index lifecycle, query construction (3 different syntaxes), hit collection (collectHits), frequency aggregation, logDice scoring (scoreHits), and collocate extraction helpers — bounded by private methods, but still multiple responsibility clusters in one class.

strengths: buildAndRankCollocates is well-extracted as a private method in BlackLabQueryExecutor, separating scoring from query execution.
registerGetHandler correctly encapsulates CORS/method-check cross-cutting concern for GET endpoints.
handleRelationQueryInternal is a good extraction that unifies handleRelationQuery and handleDependencyRelationQuery behind a single entry point.
collectHits and scoreHits in BlackLabQueryExecutor represent a meaningful phase separation for the BCQL execution pipeline.
design_coherence.issue_character: The dominant design problem is a god-class API server that accumulates all HTTP, serialization, and orchestration concerns; repeated structural patterns (parameter parsing, JSON building) resist extraction because there are no typed response or request objects.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The codebase scores below the midpoint because its largest file (988 lines) violates single-responsibility in a way that causes measurable code duplication across handlers, and its inner-class DTO arrangement forces awkward cross-module imports. The private-method decomposition within BlackLabQueryExecutor is better but still bundles multiple concerns. This is not a passable design — it requires structural refactoring to reach a healthy baseline.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer mixes server lifecycle, routing, request parsing, business dispatch, and JSON serialization in one 988-line class
evidence: 10+ private handler methods each contain their own parameter extraction, validation, executor call, and Map<String,Object> serialization — no separation of concerns between these layers.
start() both registers routes (side effect) and starts the server — acknowledged in TODO comment 595bb84c.
issues.suggestion: Extract a RequestContext/ResponseSerializer layer (or typed DTO classes) for JSON building. Move route registration to a separate registerRoutes() method. Consider splitting into RequestDispatcher + individual RequestHandler classes, one per endpoint group.

issues.summary: Parameter parsing and numeric validation block is duplicated across handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
evidence: Both handlers contain: resolveRelationAlias, grammarConfig null check, getRelation lookup, and try-catch block parsing top/min_shared/min_logdice with NumberFormatException — roughly 20 lines duplicated verbatim.
The only difference is that explore-multi also parses seeds while explore parses seed + nouns_per.
issues.suggestion: Extract a SemanticFieldParams record and a parseSemanticFieldParams(Map<String,String> params, boolean multiSeed) helper that returns the parsed record or sends a 400 and returns null.

issues.summary: Six result DTOs are inner classes of SemanticFieldExplorer, forcing the API layer to import implementation internals
evidence: WordSketchApiServer imports: SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions — 6 inner-class imports from an implementation class.
SemanticFieldExplorer Javadoc acknowledges: 'These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585)' — the known issue has not been addressed.
issues.suggestion: Move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExploreOptions, and Edge to a dedicated pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package. Update all import sites.

issues.summary: POST handlers (handleVisualRadial, handleBcqlQueryPost) re-implement OPTIONS/method-check logic already encapsulated in registerGetHandler
evidence: handleVisualRadial: 'if (OPTIONS.equalsIgnoreCase(method)) { sendOptionsResponse(...); return; } if (!requireMethod(exchange, POST)) { return; }' — 4 lines that mirror registerGetHandler's pattern.
registerGetHandler exists precisely to centralize this cross-cutting concern, but POST endpoints bypass it because it hardcodes 'GET'.
issues.suggestion: Add a registerHandler(server, path, method, handler) overload to registerGetHandler (renaming it) that takes the HTTP method as a parameter; use it for both GET and POST registrations.

root_causes: No typed request/response DTO layer: all endpoints build Map<String,Object> by hand, making extraction non-trivial and discouraging reuse.
Result model classes were prototyped as inner classes and were never migrated out — acknowledged as tech debt but not prioritized.
